km.Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales: លលែនឌីប៊ី, ខារម៉ាtហេនស៊ីរe, វ៉ាឡិស

zh.Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威爾士卡馬特史爾利安迪比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Rhys ap David Howel於大約1545年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾利安迪比時，他的父親John Ap Hywel17歲，母親Cecily Ferch Rhys21歲。他和Gwenllian ferch Jenkin至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales: 威尔士卡馬特史爾利安迪比
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rhys ap David Howel于大约1545年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾利安迪比时，他的父亲John Ap Hywel17岁，母亲Cecily Ferch Rhys21岁。他和Gwenllian ferch Jenkin至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Howel
meanings: The English form of the Welsh name Hywel.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When Rhys ap David Howel was born about 1545, in Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, John Ap Hywel, was 17 and his mother, Cecily Ferch Rhys, was 21. He had at least 1 daughter with Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rhys ap David Howel est né environ 1545, à Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, John Ap Hywel, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Cecily Ferch Rhys, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rhys ap David Howel um 1545 in Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Ap Hywel 17 und seine Mutter Cecily Ferch Rhys 21. Er hatte mit Gwenllian ferch Jenkin mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap David Howel è nato il circa 1545 nel Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, John Ap Hywel, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, Cecily Ferch Rhys, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.

ja.Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Wales: ウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランディビ
ja.lifeSummary: Rhys ap David Howelが約1545年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアランディビで生まれた時，父John Ap Hywelは17歳，母Cecily Ferch Rhysは21歳であった。彼はGwenllian ferch Jenkinとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Rhys ap David Howel(이)가 대략 1545년 웨일스 카마테실르 랜디비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Ap Hywel(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecily Ferch Rhys(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Gwenllian ferch Jenkin(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap David Howel nasceu aproximadamente 1545, em Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, John Ap Hywel, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Cecily Ferch Rhys, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rhys ap David Howel родился около 1545 г. в местности лландиби, кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени John Ap Hywel было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily Ferch Rhys, 21. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rhys ap David Howel nació aproximadamente 1545, en Llandybie, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, John Ap Hywel, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Cecily Ferch Rhys, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Gwenllian ferch Jenkin.
